summary: Dr. Robert Langer's 2022 commencement address at Alfred University, where he shares his career journey from rejecting lucrative oil industry jobs to pioneering drug delivery and mRNA vaccines. He emphasizes the importance of following one's passion, persevering through massive rejection, and above all, treating people with kindness.

principles.statement: Follow your passion and optimize for impact, not money.
principles.rationale: Choosing a career based on what you truly care about allows you to dream big and ultimately change the world for the better.
principles.source_quote: follow your passion choose something that you think you'll really love don't do something just for the money do it for what you really care about

principles.statement: Never give up in the face of roadblocks and rejection.
principles.rationale: Breakthroughs require enduring immense skepticism, failed experiments, and institutional rejection. Success comes to those who refuse to quit.
principles.source_quote: you know when you do things like this and whatever you do i'm sure you're going to run into roadblocks everybody does and no matter how bad things look i hope you don't give up

principles.statement: Kindness and respect are the ultimate drivers of success.
principles.rationale: Building a successful lab, company, or career ultimately comes down to treating people well; it is the most important human trait.
principles.source_quote: no matter what you do with your life career-wise there's nothing more important than being a good caring kind human being

mental_models.name: The "Impossible" Consensus
mental_models.description: Viewing widespread scientific skepticism not as a stop sign, but as a signal of a massive breakthrough opportunity. When the consensus says something cannot be done, proving them wrong creates paradigm-shifting innovation.
mental_models.example: Pursuing nanoparticle drug delivery for large molecules (like RNA) despite older scientists comparing it to "walking through a brick wall" and telling him it was impossible.

heuristics: Dream big dreams that you hope can change the world and make it a better place.
Treat people the way you hope they would treat you.

signature_quotes.text: follow your passion choose something that you think you'll really love don't do something just for the money do it for what you really care about
signature_quotes.context: Advice to graduating students on choosing their career path.

signature_quotes.text: no matter what you do with your life career-wise there's nothing more important than being a good caring kind human being
signature_quotes.context: Concluding advice on the secret to his lab's success.

signature_quotes.text: i actually found over 200 different ways to get this to not work but finally i discovered some ways where i could modify certain materials make them into tiny particles and and get it to work
signature_quotes.context: Describing his early struggles developing nanoparticle drug delivery.

anti_patterns.description: Giving up when experts tell you your idea is impossible.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Experts base their opinions on current paradigms. If you listen to the consensus that a new approach is 'impossible,' you will miss out on creating breakthrough innovations like mRNA vaccines.

anti_patterns.description: Taking the easy, high-paying job just because it is available.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It leads to unfulfilling work that doesn't align with your desire to make a positive impact on the world, as seen when he rejected 20 lucrative oil company offers to pursue low-paying medical research.
